top_risk: Claim (b) is unmeasured: no fishing/mining earn-rate baseline exists, so encounter rewards drawn from that pool could materially undercut those games' earn rates undetected.
cheapest_evidence: Ship the slice logging encounter mint AND fishing+mining coins/items per active user per day over the same window, then compute the encounter-mint / fishing-mining-earn ratio; claim (a)'s tuning is already settled by the in-repo deterministic sim.
report:
## (1) What this really is / core claim
A passive engagement mechanic: a debounced per-channel message counter that fires a 'Wild Encounter' spawn (embed + Claim button) at a threshold, awarding the first valid claimer via an audited workflow. It is the repo's first message-triggered game event and deliberately reuses existing infrastructure (economy_service coins, fishing/mining item catalogue, a new GAME_ENCOUNTERS XP key) rather than minting new currency. Two load-bearing claims: (a) some parameter set keeps encounters rare-but-visible across low/med/high traffic while making paced-spam farming unprofitable; (b) drawing rewards from the fishing/mining pool doesn't materially undercut those games' earn rates. Status: idea ALREADY simulated in-repo (wild_encounter_spawn_sim.py, REPORT.md), verdict needs-more-evidence.

## (2) Strongest failure mode
Reward inflation (claim b). Sim does NOT settle it: no live earn-rate baseline exists for fishing/mining. Sim reports only ABSOLUTE mint (~6.8/24.0/35.0 claims per 8h window low/med/high) but cannot say whether that undercuts source games. Secondary: reflex-claim bot not modeled; only binding guarantee is analytic per-claimer cooldown cap (4 claims/hr at cd=900s), which must be enforced server-side at the Claim callback.

## (3) Falsify / cheapest evidence
Claim (a) effectively settled: deterministic, stdlib-only, 5-seed, 946-self-check sim. Defaults: threshold=24, debounce=30s, cooldown=900s. Claim (b) only falsifiable on live data: log per-spawn, per-claim, per-channel/day mint totals, AND fishing+mining earned per active user/day. Residual gaps: EST traffic tiers, reflex-bot timing.

## (4) Cost/complexity vs payoff
Low-to-moderate: no new currency, reuses services; build surface a single Encounters cog plus audited claim workflow. Complexity in atomic exactly-once claim, server-side cooldown, off-by-default opt-in. Payoff: passive engagement with bounded auditable mint. Tuning risk retired by sim; outstanding cost is telemetry.

## (5) Smallest shippable slice
Ship defaults with ALL guardrails: one-live-spawn-per-channel, off-by-default + opt-in + allow-list, server-side cooldown at callback, atomic exactly-once claim. Treat reward VALUES as PROVISIONAL. Primary job beyond mechanic: emit telemetry that closes claim (b).
